---
title: "Uploading A Sample Bar Code Document"
---

Uploading a sample document containing a bar code helps you to configure the bar code profile settings and ensures the bar codes are detected correctly. The supported file formats are JPG and PNG.

To upload a sample document containing a bar code:

1.  On the Create Bar Code Profile screen, click browse to upload a sample document. The File dialog box is displayed.
Alternatively, you can drag and drop the relevant bar code image or file in the Drag & drop files to Import box.

The bar code type, value, and scanning area details are automatically generated based on the bar code area you specify.

2.  In the File dialog box, select the bar code file you want to upload and click Open. The Select bar code area dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  From the list of bar codes available in the uploaded document, select the bar code type to use for separation.
When selecting the bar code type, consider the following:

-
All the detected bar codes are highlighted in yellow. The selected bar code is highlighted in blue.

-
If you are unsure of the exact location of the bar code on the page, you can select Scan whole image to configure the bar code separation profile to scan the entire page to detect the bar code (see step 4 for details).

-
If you want to manually set the bar code area, type, and value instead of using the automatically detected settings from the sample document, you can select Manual as the type (see step 5 for details).

-
Depending on your selections, a nudgeable box is displayed around the automatically detected bar code or the area you draw manually. You can resize the box by clicking and dragging its corners. For accurate bar code detection during scanning, it is recommended that you make the size of the nudgeable box larger than the bar code.

4.  To configure a profile to scan the entire page for bar codes, select Scan whole image. Selecting this option sets the Starting Position From Left and Starting Position From Top fields to 0 and the Width and Height fields to 100.
5.  To manually select the bar code area on the page, select Manual. This allows you to draw a nudgeable box around the region of the page where the application needs to search for a bar code.
When using the Manual option, also note the following:

-
The Manual option is selected by default if no bar codes are detected.

-
After uploading the sample file, you must select the appropriate bar code type from the Bar Code Type drop-down list on the Create Separation Profile screen.

- The nudgeable box appears on the first click. Click again to resize the nudgeable box.
6.  Click Save.  Note:
The sample image you have uploaded is not stored or reused for future configurations; only the settings that have been created with the image are stored. You must upload the image again if you want to use it to edit the same profile.
